item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents strongly oppose the statement throughout. Agent A begins with 'TIDAK SETUJU' and ends by rejecting general priority for society over individual rights; Agent B also consistently rejects blanket social priority. Both acknowledge that individual rights can be narrowly limited for public safety or health, but this is present as a qualification rather than a position shift. No visible flip; the debate shows shared anti-overreach framing rather than drift.
